title: Logic Apps Expression Functions — Complete Reference With Working Examples
applies_to: Expression syntax for actions and conditions

confirmed_facts.fact: All expressions use @{...} syntax inside action inputs (string interpolation) or @expression() when the entire value is an expression
In workflow definition JSON, expressions are bare strings starting with @ — e.g., "@triggerBody()" not "@{triggerBody()}"
To include a literal @ in a string, escape it with @@

implementation_notes:
=== REFERENCING DATA ===
triggerBody() — full trigger output body
triggerOutputs() — full trigger outputs including headers
body('actionName') — output body of a named action
outputs('actionName') — full outputs of a named action
items('ForEachName') — current item in a For_each loop
item() — current item when inside an implicit loop (e.g., Select)
actions('actionName').outputs.body — alternative body access
variables('varName') — get variable value
parameters('paramName') — get workflow parameter
trigger().outputs.headers['headerName'] — trigger response header
workflow().run.name — current run ID
workflow().name — workflow name

=== STRING FUNCTIONS ===
concat('a', 'b', 'c') → 'abc'
substring('hello', 1, 3) → 'ell'
indexOf('hello', 'l') → 2
lastIndexOf('hello', 'l') → 3
replace('hello', 'l', 'r') → 'herro'
split('a,b,c', ',') → ['a','b','c']
join(['a','b','c'], ',') → 'a,b,c'
toLower('ABC') → 'abc'
toUpper('abc') → 'ABC'
trim('  hi  ') → 'hi'
startsWith('hello', 'he') → true
endsWith('hello', 'lo') → true
contains('hello', 'ell') → true
length('hello') → 5
nthIndexOf('a.b.c', '.', 1) → 1
slice('hello', 1, 3) → 'el'
chunk('abcdef', 2) → ['ab','cd','ef']

=== CONVERSION FUNCTIONS === int('42') → 42 float('3.14') → 3.14 string(42) → '42' bool('true') → true json('{"a":1}') → {a:1} xml('1') → XML object array('hello') → ['hello'] createArray('a', 'b') → ['a','b'] base64('text') → base64 string base64ToString('dGV4dA==') → 'text' dataUri('text') → data URI dataUriToBinary(uri) → binary uriComponent('a b') → 'a%20b' uriComponentToString('a%20b') → 'a b' decodeBase64('dGV4dA==') → 'text' decodeDataUri(uri) → binary

=== DATE AND TIME FUNCTIONS ===
utcNow() → '2026-04-11T12:00:00.0000000Z'
utcNow('yyyy-MM-dd') → '2026-04-11'
addDays(utcNow(), -7) → 7 days ago
addHours(utcNow(), 12) → 12 hours from now
addMinutes(utcNow(), 30)
addSeconds(utcNow(), 60)
addToTime(utcNow(), 1, 'Month')
subtractFromTime(utcNow(), 1, 'Day')
startOfDay(utcNow()) → midnight today
startOfMonth(utcNow()) → first of month
startOfHour(utcNow())
dayOfWeek(utcNow()) → 0-6 (Sunday=0)
dayOfMonth(utcNow()) → 1-31
dayOfYear(utcNow()) → 1-366
formatDateTime(utcNow(), 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ')
convertFromUtc(utcNow(), 'Pacific Standard Time', 'yyyy-MM-dd')
convertToUtc('2026-04-11', 'Pacific Standard Time')
ticks(utcNow()) → ticks count
parseDateTime('2026-04-11') → datetime

=== MATH AND LOGICAL FUNCTIONS ===
add(1, 2) → 3
sub(5, 2) → 3
mul(3, 4) → 12
div(10, 3) → 3 (integer division)
mod(10, 3) → 1
min(1, 2, 3) → 1
max(1, 2, 3) → 3
rand(1, 100) → random integer
and(true, true) → true
or(true, false) → true
not(false) → true
if(true, 'yes', 'no') → 'yes'
equals(1, 1) → true
less(1, 2) → true
lessOrEquals(1, 1) → true
greater(2, 1) → true
greaterOrEquals(2, 2) → true
coalesce(null, null, 'default') → 'default'

=== JSON PATH AND OBJECT ACCESS ===
body('action')?['property'] — safe property access (returns null if missing)
body('action')['property'] — throws if property missing
body('action')?['nested']?['deep'] — chained safe access
setProperty(object, 'key', 'value') — returns new object with key set
removeProperty(object, 'key') — returns new object without key
addProperty(object, 'key', 'value') — returns new object with key added
xpath(xml, '/root/element') — XPath query on XML
caveats: Expression functions are case-sensitive: utcNow() works, UtcNow() does not
In JSON definition, use bare @ for full expressions: "@triggerBody()" — not "@{triggerBody()}" (interpolation syntax is only for string embedding)
The ?[] safe navigation only works with bracket notation, not dot notation
division with div() is INTEGER division — use float() conversion for decimal results
formatDateTime uses .NET format strings, NOT Python strftime: yyyy not %Y, MM not %m, dd not %d, HH not %H
coalesce() skips null values but NOT empty strings — use if(empty(...), ..., ...) for empty string handling
